summary: As a key resource of the Learning & Development team, the Learning & Development Specialist will manage end-to-end the apprentices and interns programmes at EFG, ensuring a best-in-class onboarding and development experience, and provide support and coordinate selected global learning initiatives.
The resource will interact with all HR functional areas and expected to become a reference person within the organization on the assigned activities.
This is an excellent opportunity for someone willing to work in an international, fast-paced and client-oriented HR team within a global private banking group.
Learning & Development Program Delivery (Apprentices & Interns)
responsibilities: Design, implement, and continuously improve apprentices and interns programmes, ensuring alignment with EFG’s standards and local requirements.
Manage the end-to-end hiring process for apprentices in Switzerland and interns globally, partnering with line managers and HR Business Partners/local HR.
Coordinate and facilitate onboarding and training sessions to build readiness and engagement for apprentices.
Prepare reports and feedback surveys to measure programme effectiveness; analyse insights to assess impact and identify opportunities for improvement.
Provide technical support for training-related topics, ensuring effective use of the company’s learning tools and systems.
Coordinate selected global learning initiatives, supporting design, rollout, communications, and logistics in partnership with the L&D team.

3+ of relevant experience in early career talent acquisition or development within structured HR departments of international companies

Strong commitment to people development and delivering outstanding customer service
International mindset and experience working in multicultural environments

Strong communication skills, with the ability to clearly and effectively structure key messages
Exceptional organizational skills; able to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and deliver in a dynamic and demanding environment

sections.heading: Our Company
paragraphs: EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

sections.heading: Our Purpose and Mission
paragraphs: Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

sections.heading: Role
paragraphs: Business support to the Global Head of Wealth Planning to the realisation of Wealth Planning’s growth ambitions in Assets under Management, Net New Assets (NNA) and Revenue targets, while driving execution and tracking of key strategic initiatives.

summary: The International Payroll Specialist will be a key resource within an evolving global payroll structure. The role ensures accurate salary processing, full compliance with local and international regulations, and effective collaboration with internal and external partners to guarantee efficient and compliant payroll operations.

This position will primarily focus on EFG locations outside Switzerland and will serve as the main point of contact for payroll‑related matters across these locations. At the same time, the role will also be involved in payroll operations for Switzerland, providing support and backup to the team as needed. This role will provide guidance on payroll topics, including allowances, tax regulations, social security and reporting requirements, supporting the organisation in maintaining high standards of compliance and service quality.

The role also offers the opportunity to contribute to the implementation of a global payroll platform for employees of EFG international locations and to progressively take ownership of multi‑country payroll operations from Switzerland
responsibilities: Manage the global payroll process from Switzerland for selected international locations, ensuring accurate and timely salary payments.
Support and act as backup for payroll activities for Switzerland‑based employees.
Coordinate with local payroll providers, ensuring compliance with tax, social security and labour regulations in each jurisdiction.
Collaborate with HR and Finance to align processes, ensure accurate interfaces and resolve issues.
Act as first point of contact for employee payroll queries, providing timely and accurate support and maintaining high data quality across locations.
Prepare and review payroll reports (salaries, contributions, tax obligations) and support internal and external audits with required documentation.
Drive initiatives to review, streamline and centralise HR/payroll processes globally, performing regular checks on payroll outputs to ensure quality and compliance.
requirements: Proven experience in multi‑country payroll management, ideally within an international or financial services environment.
Strong knowledge of international payroll processes and compliance requirements (tax, social security, labour law) across multiple jurisdictions.
Experience with payroll platforms and HR systems, with strong attention to data quality and process reliability.
Structured, detail‑oriented and highly reliable, with strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple deadlines in a dynamic and demanding environment.
Comfortable working in a regulated and international context, with high levels of integrity and discretion in handling confidential data.
International mindset and experience working in multicultural environments.
Excellent interpersonal and collaboration skills, with a proactive and positive attitude, energy and intellectual curiosity.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to provide clear explanations to non‑specialist stakeholders.
Fluent in English; knowledge of national languages, particularly French and Italian, is a strong asset.

Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

sections.heading: Specification of Core Function
paragraphs: Our team delivers solutions and provides support for internal and third-party systems in place for the Investment Client Solution (ICS) business. Support, business analysis, testing and development is provided for all business lines of the ICS including Advisory, Discretionary and New Capital Funds. The main aspect of this role is Support and Business Analyst functions. The role involves interaction with a wide range of end users from all aspects of the business (e.g. Front office, Middle office, Back office, Risk and Compliance as well as IT). The role involves providing support for Production critical issues, understanding and challenging business change requirements, testing changes, co-ordinating delivery workstreams, devising workable efficient scalable solutions to meet business requirements.

summary: We’re hiring a senior FX Specialist in Zurich to develop and deliver a strong FX advisory capability for our private banking clients.
This is a newly created role, focused on building relationships with CROs (Client Relationship Officers) and engaging clients with relevant and timely FX solutions.

If you are expert and experienced in FX markets, with extensive proficiency in all FX tools, comfortable working independently, and motivated by the chance to shape something from the ground up — this could be the right next step in your career.
Why EFG, Why This Role?
responsibilities: As subject matter expert the resource will engage directly with private banking clients and work alongside CROs to deliver tailored FX advice and solutions.
Manage highly divergent audience at an high level of complexity
Complement the efforts of CROs by bringing extensive market expertise and product knowledge to client conversations.
Offer tailored solutions across the full range of FX products, aligned with client objectives, risk profiles, and prevailing market conditions.
Take ownership of the full trade lifecycle: you will anticipate business needs and offer unique solutions - from opportunity identification to post-trade support.
Monitor macroeconomic and market developments and translate insights into actionable advice.
Be the key enablers in expanding FX advisory within the Switzerland booking center and strengthening internal awareness of the offering.
Being accountable for delivery of tactical business targets that are financially measurable and discipline‑level impact as a norm .
New role, clear focus: the resource will have space to define and develop this function , with the backing of a strong platform.

High visibility: work directly with CROs and senior leaders across the business. You will be the subject matter expert managing diverse and senior stakeholders internally and externally to convey complex information.
Environment: Agile, international, and client-focused, without unnecessary layers.
requirements: 15 years of experience in FX advisory in a private banking or wealth management environment.
Deep knowledge of FX markets and macroeconomic trends.
Comfortable interacting with clients and CROs in a thoughtful, clear, and solution-oriented way.
Independent and autonomous in seeking opportunities and acts on them.
Strong attention to detail, especially around execution and follow-up.
Fluency in English required; German or French or Italian language is considered as a plus.

Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

sections.heading: Role
paragraphs: The Insurance Center of Excellence (CoE) serves as the group centre of competence on the insurance offering of Wealth Planning team. The CoE on insurance manages the insurance broker network, provides expertise, governance, training and oversight to ensure insurance offering is rolled out consistently at group and regional level. It supports wealth planners and CROs and other internal units with technical knowledge, product evaluation and compliance rigor. The CoE ensures that insurance offering and referral framework of EFG is technically sound, client-appropriate, compliant with regulatory and tax requirements, and aligned with the bank’s overall wealth planning framework.

process: Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

sections.heading: Introduction of the team
paragraphs: Main responsibilities
bullets: At EFG we want to create value fom data. Within our Data Office department, we’re seeking a highly skilled and motivated AI Software Engineer to join our Machine Learning & GenAI team. You will embark on building a new, scalable AI platform and design, build, and deploy AI-driven systems that deliver measurable business impact.
This is an excellent opportunity to make a significant impact in a growing organization committed to delivering an outstanding digital banking experience for our clients.
1) Platform and Architecture
Design and build a hybrid (on-prem / on-cloud) AI/ML platform to run AI use cases at scale (feature stores, model registry, experimentation, evaluation, observability).
Define and implement secure, reliable inference and training architectures, including vector search and RAG components where applicable.
Provide platform support for embeddings, vector databases, and AI agentic communication protocols to enable grounded, interoperable AI workflows.
Document machine learning processes, system architecture, and operational runbooks for reproducibility and knowledge sharing.
2) Model Development & Evaluation
Collaborate on training, fine-tuning, and optimizing models (LLMs, NLP, recommendations), including LoRA/PEFT when relevant.
Implement guardrails and prompt strategies to reduce hallucinations and improve safety and consistency, and support agentic workflows.
Establish evaluation frameworks for RAG and LLM systems.
3) Software Development & MLOps
Own end-to-end software development of AI services and APIs (from design and coding to testing and deployment).
Automate build, test, and deployment using CI/CD pipelines; manage model/version releases via model registries.
Implement continuous monitoring for deployed AI systems.
4) Product & Stakeholder Collaboration
Work closely with Business Users, Product Owners, Business Engineers, Data Managers, Data Scientists, and technology teams to understand AI/ML use cases, requirements, and success metrics.
Partner with Data Scientists to iterate on prototypes and convert them into robust, scalable production services.
Translate emerging GenAI/Agentic AI capabilities into actionable product opportunities and reusable components for the Bank.
5) Security, Privacy, and Compliance
Ensure all AI/ML solutions meet bank-wide data and AI guidelines and standards, including data protection, cyber security, and responsible AI practices.
Embed privacy-by-design, access controls, encryption, and auditability across data flows and model operations.
Collaborate with Risk, Security, and Compliance to align with SOC 2, GDPR/CCPA, and internal governance.

sections.heading: 1) Education
bullets: Advanced degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, or related.

sections.heading: 2) Must-Have
bullets: Extensive knowledge of ML/AI frameworks: PyTorch or TensorFlow; Hugging Face ecosystem; LangChain/LlamaIndex or equivalent for orchestration, data structures, data modeling, and software architecture.
Practical LLM experience: prompt engineering, fine-tuning/LoRA, embeddings, vector databases (FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate), RAG patterns.
Solid programming skills in Python, R or Java/Scala, hands on experience in SQL, ETL tool and Linux and Control-M & Terraform knowledge are a plus.
Prior experience deploying applications on cloud environments (Azure); familiarity with hybrid on‑prem/cloud setups.
Experience building production-grade services and APIs (REST/gRPC), cloud-native (AWS/GCP/Azure), containers (Docker), and orchestration (Openshift, Kubernetes).
MLOps foundations: experiment tracking (MLflow/W&B), model registries, CI/CD, model monitoring, feature stores.
Ability to monitor, debug, and maintain CI/CD pipelines that feed into production deployments (GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/Azure DevOps).
Data engineering proficiency: SQL, data modeling, ETL/ELT, and working with warehouses/lakes (Snowflake, BigQuery, S3/Delta).
Ability to work in a SCRUM/Agile environment with a focus on delivery and stakeholder collaboration.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities; results- and detail-oriented with strong written and verbal communication.
Experience in the Banking Or Wealth Management Industry is an advantage

sections.heading: 3) Nice-to-Have
paragraphs: Our Values
bullets: Experience deploying and optimizing open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Mixtral) on GPUs; quantization (INT8/4), tensor/Flash attention.
Knowledge of retrieval systems (BM25, hybrid search), semantic caching, and structured tool use/agents.
Evaluation expertise for LLMs: rubric-based grading, golden sets, adversarial testing, and A/B experimentation.
Security practices for AI applications: prompt injection defenses, output filtering, content moderation, and red-teaming.
Contributions to open-source AI projects or published work.
Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.
Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

sections.heading: About EFG
paragraphs: EFG International is a global private banking group, serving clients in over 40 locations worldwide. As EFG continues to grow, we are building a talent pool for future Client Services Officer (CSO) positions in Geneva. Joining our talent pool means you will be among the first considered for upcoming opportunities, contributing to our dynamic and inclusive environment.
EFG is committed to equity, inclusion, and mutual respect. We value diversity as a driver of innovation and better decision-making, and we strive to be an employer of choice.

sections.heading: Our Purpose and Mission
paragraphs: Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.
We offer personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success depends on our people and our partnerships with clients and communities.

sections.heading: Candidate Communication: What to Expect
paragraphs: As part of our talent pool, your profile will be reviewed for a variety of CSO opportunities as they become available. The private banking market is dynamic, and the requirements for each role may shift in response to client needs, regulatory changes, or business priorities. We value candidates who are adaptable and open to evolving responsibilities.

If your profile matches the requirements for a specific opening, you may be pre-selected and invited to complete an additional questionnaire. This helps us better understand your experience, skills, and career preferences, ensuring a strong fit for both you and EFG.

summary: The IT Treasury and Trading team delivers efficient, effective support and IT solutions for applications and processes in client order execution, trading, treasury, and lending/collateral management. The team comprises both functional and technical experts supporting a Trading/Treasury room of approximately 50 traders. This role ensures continuity in client services and drives ongoing improvements in deliverable quality.
responsibilities: Run the Bank: end-to-end support on the IT trading platforms used for Securities Lending & Collateral, Islamic Finance, Securities and Derivatives execution and trading, spanning from application maintenance to analysis and resolution on daily incidents, liaising with external vendors where applicable.

Run the Bank: act as entry point to trading desk for broader issues that require liaising with other IT departments to cover the full stack (core banking and other linked applications, IT infa server, database, network, …) or with Operations teams (Securities, Derivatives, FX back office, middle office)

Change the Bank: coordinate and execute various IT projects aimed at evolving or upgrading the IT trading platforms, either developed and released internally or by application providers: collection of business needs, solution design and documentation, development where applicable and/or vendor coordination, creation of test cases catalogue and execution of IT tests, support to user acceptance test, rollout and post production support
Change the Bank: development of departmental applications aimed at covering gaps of the existing IT applications, mainly in the area of system integration and reporting
Change the Bank: management of tools for the automation of health checks on trading IT platforms to eventually guarantee the service to internal clients
requirements: A university degree, preferably in computer science, finance or an equivalent educational background
Very good knowledge of programming languages (Java; Pyton; C++, SQL, Powershell, …), application server (Windows mainly, Linux) and database systems (Oracle, MS SQL)
Good knowledge of financial products (Forex, Securities, Derivatives, Sec Lending) and main bank processes (order/execution on the market of the plain vanilla products)
Very good analytic skill, tech curiosity, proactivity in identifying the best tech solution to a business need
Capability to work under constant pressure
Flexibility (request to work by shift; during bank-holiday or Saturday)
Capability to integrate in a wider IT team and good team player
Good communication skills (Italian-English are a must, French is a plus)
benefits: Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

responsibilities: Overall responsibility for the administrative management, technical-accounting and business activities of Fondazione
Define the financial and actuarial regulations and parameters underlying the plan in order to achieve long-term financial equilibrium
Administrative and operational management of the pension fund, including enrolment of members, management of contributions and benefits.
Supervises the establishment of the Annual Report, the accounting and actuarial reporting to the foundation board and the supervisory bodies as well as the reporting according to IAS 19 to EFG SA
Coordination with external bodies (actuarial consultants, auditors, supervisory authorities) and service providers (custodian banks, asset managers).
Supervision of pension fund investments, in accordance with the investment regulations and risk criteria.
Supervision and control of annual accounts, financial statements and documentation required by supervisory bodies.
Supporting the Board of Foundation in defining the pension fund's strategy, ensuring the financial stability of the foundation
Managing communications to members, including information on benefits and regulatory changes.
Monitoring regulatory developments in the field of pensions and updating internal procedures.
Supervises the internal control system
Ensure constant collaboration with the human resources department of EFG SA, agreeing procedures, identifying synergies and jointly analysing common topics
Prepare and coordinate the meetings of the foundation board and investment committee, drawing up the relevant minutes
Actively provide pension advice to all insured persons (active employees and pensioners.

Excellent organisational, analytical and interpersonal skills.

Managerial competences.

sections.heading: Job Summary
paragraphs: The Pension Fund Manager is in charge of managing and administering the company pension fund, ensuring compliance with current regulations and protection of the interests of the employees of affiliated companies against the economic consequences of old age, death, and disability. He/she coordinates all operational, financial, and administrative activities of the pension fund, in line with the foundation regulations, policies and legal provisions.

sections.heading: Working Relationships
paragraphs: The Administration of the Foundation is composed of a small team, where collaboration between the Pension Fund Manager and all other roles of the Team is essential.

The role implies frequent interactions with Board members, external auditors, actuaries, human resources office, reinsurer, investment advisory, responsible of investment mandates, relationship manager of the Custodian Bank.

sections.heading: Decision-Making & Autonomy
paragraphs: Autonomous and structured management of all tasks, with a global overview of the entire operations of the Foundation.

Plans and organizes activities quickly, recognizing priorities, formulating solutions appropriate to the circumstances. Communicates his views to colleagues in a timely manner, seeking to promote transparency, circulation of information within the team and process efficiency.

sections.heading: Working Conditions
paragraphs: Work in EFG Office Lugano with frequent travel to EFG offices or to service providers (Actuary, Investment Controller, cooperating financial institutions, …) in Geneva or Zurich.

summary:
General Info

  • Department: Back Office Cash Reconciliation Specialist
  • Work time Percentage: 100%
  • Location: Geneva
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

We are seeking a skilled Back Office Cash Reconciliation Specialist to join our team in Geneva. This position offers the opportunity to contribute to a dynamic environment where precision, collaboration, and proactive problem-solving are highly valued. Main duties include

  • Cash Reconciliation
    • Perform reconciliation of cash correspondents for all EFG entities
    • Manually input statements from correspondents not automatically reconciled
    • Monitor, check, and investigate cash differences on a daily basis
      • Establish and maintain a list of pending cash amounts
    • Manage treatment and follow-up of current files
    • Claim and recover missing statements from counterparties
  • Investigation on Customer Wires
    • Demonstrate strong knowledge of cash transfer activities (e.g., MT103, MT202, etc.)
    • Understand the roles of each counterparty involved in wire payments
    • Draft and send SWIFT messages (e.g., MTx99, MTx95, MTx96, etc.) as needed
    • Take responsibility for managing investigations from start to finish
    • Apply familiarity with compliance requirements throughout investigations
    • Communicate effectively in English, both orally and in writing
Skills and experience
  • Minimum of 2 years’ experience in cash reconciliation and/or investigation of customer wire transfers
  • General understanding of Back-Office operations within banking
  • French mother tongue and fluent English (spoken and written); additional languages are an asset
  • Knowledge of GTMatch, GTExchange, and Globus is advantageous
  • Proficient in MS Office suite
  • Solution-oriented, pragmatic, and precise
  • Strong team player, comfortable working in open space environments
  • Resilient under pressure, flexible, and adaptable
  • Proactive with excellent attention to detail
Our Values
  • Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
  • Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
  • Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
  • Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
  • Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.
  1. Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.
Application
  1. Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

Within Risk & Compliance IT, you are responsible for key applications (primarily from sas.com) and play a hands-on role on the technical solutions: support of the application, data models, reports, data flows and functional enhancements.
The role is evenly split between hands‑on technical activities (around 50%) and business analysis / project responsibilities (around 50%).
You combine a solid understanding of Risk & Compliance topics with strong technical skills (SQL / SAS / data modelling), while working closely with business stakeholders and external providers.
responsibilities: Act as IT Application Owner for Risk & Compliance solutions.
Analyse business needs and translate them into concrete technical solutions.
Design, adapt and optimise data models, extractions and reports (SQL, SAS).
Manage application changes end-to-end: impact analysis, specifications, coordination with provider teams, UAT and go‑live.
Provide advanced day‑to‑day support to users; investigate and resolve issues, escalating to provider or internal teams when necessary.
Ensure the quality, integrity and security of data used for risk and compliance reporting.
benefits: Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges as well as seeking continuous improvement.
Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results.
Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence.
Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset.
Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

sections.heading: Profile
bullets: University degree in IT, Computer Science or a related field.
Proven experience in a banking or financial services environment, ideally within Risk or Compliance.
Strong hands‑on skills in SQL and relational databases (writing and optimising queries, designing data models).
Practical experience with SAS for reporting and/or data processing; knowledge of SAS Asset & Liability Risk Management is a strong plus.
Comfortable working with Windows Server and Linux environments.
Experience with IT service management and full software lifecycle.
Able to work independently, manage requests end‑to‑end and collaborate with distributed teams.
Excellent communication skills in English; French and or Italian is an asset.

summary: Cloud & Infrastructure Design and Implementation
responsibilities:
Cloud & Infrastructure Design and Implementation

  • Design, build and deploy cloud, hybrid and on‑premises solutions in line with the bank’s IT strategy and architecture standards.
Participate in the design and implementation of IT infrastructure projects related to systems (servers, storage, virtualization, desktops).
Contribute to the definition and implementation of the technology direction, vision and strategy to enable and accelerate cloud technology adoption.

M365 & Azure Engineering

  • Act as a subject-matter expert for M365 (Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, security & compliance features, etc.) and contribute to Azure-based solutions.
Lead or support application readiness assessments, environment design and configuration for M365 and Azure workloads.
Perform prototyping of new environments, server builds and configuration to validate solutions before production rollout.

Cloud Adoption & Optimization

  • Drive and support cloud adoption initiatives, including:
Application and infrastructure readiness assessments
Data and workload migration to M365/Azure
Performance tuning and optimization of cloud environments
Ensure solutions are secure, resilient, scalable and compliant with banking and regulatory requirements.

Automation & Operational Excellence

  • Develop, enhance and maintain automation for infrastructure components (e.g. provisioning, configuration, monitoring, patching, lifecycle management).
Contribute to the standardization and industrialization of infrastructure services and processes.
Support continuous improvement of operational procedures, documentation and runbooks.

Collaboration & Support

  • Provide technical expertise and guidance to other IT support teams on all cloud topics (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS).
Act as an escalation point for complex incidents and problems related to M365, Azure and hybrid infrastructure.

updatedJobs.title: Operational Risk and Advisory IT and Information Security Specialist

summary: The position of Operational Risk and Advisory Specialist is within the Operational Risk Function, a critical component of the second line of defense. This role is responsible for providing comprehensive oversight of operational risk activities, with a strong emphasis on new technologies, information security, and cyber risks. The Operational Risk function engages in advisory, risk assessment, analysis, and reporting to the Bank’s governing bodies.
responsibilities:
Development, Implementation, and Execution of Risk Activities:
  • Enhance the risk management framework in relation to risk assessment and advisory, particularly in relation to new technologies, information security and cyber risks.
Collaborate with business units to integrate advanced risk management practices into their operations.

Risk Assessment and Analysis:
  • Conduct ad hoc risk assessments and analyses, especially concerning information security and cyber threats, to identify potential issues and propose efficient mitigating measures.
Perform incident analysis to determine root causes, identify mitigating actions, and ensure follow-up.

Advisory Support:
  • Provide advisory support to Business Units, including reviewing the Internal Control Framework with a focus IT and Information Security in the way they support the sustainable business development of the Bank
Offer expert advice on risk mitigation strategies, particularly related to information security and emerging technologies.

Reporting and Communication:
  • Report and present risk assessment results to the Risk Control leadership group and other relevant stakeholders, with a focus on cyber risk and technological vulnerabilities.
Prepare detailed risk reports and presentations for executive review.

Digital Solutions Deployment:
  • Actively participate in deploying new digital solutions to support the implementation of the internal control system globally, ensuring these solutions address information security and cyber risk concerns.
requirements: University degree in business administration, economics, science, information security, or a related field.
10 years of working experience, including consulting or audit at a leading professional services firm and/or in a comparable position at a market-leading bank.
Strong understanding of core banking systems, end-to-end transaction processing, and cybersecurity principles.
Proven ability to write reports and communicate effectively with a senior audience.
Excellent analytical skills and a structured approach to problem-solving and reporting.
Exceptional communication skills in English, both written and spoken.
Capacity to work autonomously and as part of a global team in a fast-paced environment.
Experience with new technologies and a deep understanding of information security and cyber risk management.

updatedJobs.title: EFG Graduate Programme 2026 - Corporate Functions - Human Resources - Lugano

summary:
We are looking for a young talent to join our EFG Graduate Programme – Corporate Functions path in Lugano, with a focus on Human Resources.

In this role, you will gain hands-on experience and valuable insight into the key areas that support and drive the success of EFG.

You will have the opportunity to work closely with our HR team, participate in talent acquisition, employee development, and performance management initiatives, and contribute to projects that shape EFG’s people strategy. This experience will provide you with a comprehensive understanding of how Human Resources enables EFG to deliver outstanding service and maintain its leadership in private banking.

How the EFG Graduate Programme works?

Starting in September 2026 and over a period of 18 months, the EFG Graduate Programme offers a challenging and exciting opportunity for recent graduates. Tailored on individual skills, interests, and competencies, the programme provides an ideal opportunity to obtain wide-ranging experience in an international and fast-growing private banking group:

  • You will start with a 2-week onboarding in Switzerland to discover EFG Mission, Vision and Culture and start building your professional network.
  • During the onboarding you will have the chance to attend specific trainings aimed at enhancing key competencies and communication skills.
  • Continuous learning opportunities to ensure you develop both personally and professionally to achieve full potential.
  • Buddy and Mentor to accompany you throughout the programme.
  • Truly global, we have positions based in Miami, London, Luxembourg, Geneva, Zurich, Lugano, Singapore and Hong Kong.

Could you be one of our future graduates?

We value diversity and we do not require specific academic majors or industry experience, but look for different mix of background, experiences and skills.

Our graduates are brilliant and enthusiastic talents, so we expect:

  • Bachelor or Master degree (completed in 2025 or on track to complete it) with outstanding academic results in a field with affinity with the selected professional path.
  • Excellent English (any other language will be an advantage)
  • International mindset
  • Strong analytical, problem solving and organisational skills
  • Forward thinking and able to suggest new ways of working to improve efficiency and competitiveness
  • Self-disciplined, committed and with strong work ethics
  • Adaptable and able to work collaboratively in a team environment with people from different backgrounds
  • Client-oriented
  • Proactive self-starter with intellectual curiosity

What will you get?

  • Dynamic work experience by rotating through different teams and business units
  • Experience an enriching international network
  • Opportunity to build expertise in your chosen field and contribute to the EFG Culture
  • Continuous development and learning opportunities on both technical and soft skills

The recruitment process for this position will begin in April 2026 and will include several interviews.

updatedJobs.title: Internship – Business Management, Global Markets & Treasury

summary:
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

As part of the Business Management team within the Global Markets & Treasury division, you will gain hands-on exposure to how strategic and operational decisions are supported through data, reporting and analysis. Working closely with senior team members, you will contribute to the production of key management information and help ensure that decision-makers have timely, accurate and insightful material at their disposal.

You will be involved in a broad range of activities, from understanding and mapping processes, managing data and dashboards to preparing presentations for senior stakeholders. This role offers an excellent opportunity to develop a solid understanding of Global Markets & Treasury business and associated financial and risk metrics, and experience how a leading financial institution operates on a day-to-day basis.

Main responsibilities

  • Understanding, documenting and assisting in improving processes for Global Markets & Treasury.
  • Manage and maintain data sources and dashboards, ensuring information is accurate, consistent and up to date.
  • Produce regular and ad hoc management reports (financials, risk metrics and KPIs) to support monitoring and decision-making.
  • Prepare clear, well-structured presentations for committees, management forums and ad hoc business requests.
  • Collaborate with colleagues across functions to gather input, validate data and ensure alignment of information.

Skills and experience

  • Graduate student available for six months for internship (required by University) – Depending on the needs of the organisation, the internship may be extended for an additional six month.
  • Strong numerical and analytical skills, with a keen attention to detail and data quality.
  • Good command of Excel and PowerPoint; familiarity with data visualisation tools (e.g. dashboards) is an advantage.
  • Ability to structure information clearly and present it in a concise, visually appealing way.
  • Strong communication skills and willingness to interact with different stakeholders.
  • Proactive, organised and able to manage multiple tasks and deadlines. Open minded, curious and eager to learn.
  • Very good knowledge of written and spoken English; additional national language is an asset.

Our Values

  • Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement.
  • Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results.
  • Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence.
  • Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset.
  • Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit..

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

General Info

  • Work time Percentage: 100%

  • Location: St Moritz

Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

To support our growing activities in St. Moritz, we are looking for a Client Service Officer who will provide high-quality administrative and client service support to our Client Relationship Officers (CROs), while also contributing to the smooth running of our front office reception and cashier operations.

This role is ideal for a service-oriented, hands-on professional who enjoys working in a dynamic, international private banking environment and who is motivated to take ownership, act proactively and deliver an outstanding client experience.

Main Responsibilities

1. Client and CRO Support

  • Support clients and CROs in their daily business activities.

  • Maintain active client contact in written and spoken German and English.

  • Provide top-quality service to clients through regular and prompt contact by phone and email.

  • Execute client account openings, closures and updates, including:

    • Conducting internet searches and basic due diligence checks.

    • Collecting and verifying corroborative documentation.

  • Prepare and follow up on client visits, including:

    • Preparing account documentation and reviewing documents.

    • Organising account statements and other supporting material.

  • Execute and control client instructions, such as:

    • Stock market transactions.

    • Payments and transfers.

    • Foreign currency trades.

  • Perform various administrative and organisational tasks, including:

    • Client reviews and periodic checks.

    • Follow-up on missing or pending documents.

    • Delivery and monitoring of hold mail.

  • Prepare marketing and client materials, such as:

    • Presentations and pitchbooks.

    • Investment proposals & portfolio reviews.

  • Actively promote paperless accounts and e-banking usage to clients.

  • Ensure full compliance with the Code of Conduct and all internal regulations (e.g. Cross Border, AML, Clear & Clean Desk Policy, Absence Management).

2. Reception and Front Office Support

  • Welcome and assist clients upon arrival, ensuring a professional and warm first impression.

  • Manage reception duties, including:

    • Answering and directing phone calls.

    • Handling appointments and coordinating meeting room bookings.

    • Prepare and serve beverages to clients and ensure a high standard of hospitality.

    • Ensure meeting rooms and client areas are tidy, well-presented and adequately equipped at all times.

    • Handle incoming and outgoing mail and deliveries, ensuring timely distribution and proper documentation.

3. Cashier and Branch Support

  • Support daily cashier operations, including:

  • Processing client deposits and withdrawals.

  • Handling basic cash and payment transactions in line with internal guidelines.

  • Collaborate closely with CROs and other team members to ensure smooth branch operations.

  • Support marketing and client events, including preparation, logistics and on-site assistance.

Skills and experience

  1. Education and Professional Background

  • Commercial apprenticeship in a bank or similar banking-related education.

  • Further education in the financial sector is an advantage.

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in a private banking or intermediary environment.

  1. Skills

  • Good understanding of financial instruments, including:

  • Equities, bonds, structured products and derivatives.

  • Solid knowledge of Compliance and AML topics relevant to private banking.

  • Confident user of Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Word).

  • Familiarity with banking systems and front-office tools is an asset.

  • Strong client service orientation with excellent interpersonal skills.

  • High level of reliability, discretion and integrity.

  • Ability to cope with pressure and tight deadlines, with a flexible approach to working hours when required.

  • Comfortable working in a small branch environment, taking on a broad range of tasks (from client administration to reception and cashier support).

  • Proficient in written and spoken German and English (essential).

  • Italian is a strong plus; any additional languages are an advantage.

Our Values

  • Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

  • Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

  • Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

  • Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

  • Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

  1. Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Application

  1. Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

sections.heading: Jon Description
paragraphs:
This role sits at the heart of our private banking risk and control framework, acting as a key partner to Client Relationship Officers and senior management. Ensuring that client onboarding, lifecycle management and transactional activities are conducted in line with internal standards and local regulatory expectations. Combining strong technical expertise in compliance and due diligence with the ability to influence and guide front-office teams, the role provides independent challenge, clear advice and pragmatic solutions on complex and high‑risk situations. The successful candidate will play a visible part in strengthening our first line of defence, enhancing the quality and consistency of controls, and contributing to a culture of robust risk management across the platform.

Main responsibilities
Support the Client Relationship Officers in proactively identifying, managing and mitigating risks arising from day-to-day business activities
Coordinate the control activity in line with the Global Private Banking Control Blueprint, though aligned to local regulatory requirements
Perform due diligence on prospective and existing client relationships
Approve funds and securities transfers, credit granting, overrides and transactions involving sanctions sensitivity
Distribute market knowledge and best practices among Client Relationship Officers, Client Service Officers and Account Managers
Build trusted working relationships with Senior Management and CROs

Contribute effectively not only to core duties but also in the additional responsibilities including deputizing the HOPBS, reviewing complex cases, and presenting confidently to various senior Management stakeholders.
Being the main point of CRO Contact for complex and High-Risk cases
Be the FLOD Single Point of Contact (SPOC) and gatekeeper when it comes to matters related to PEP Classification and Declassification
Ensure timely completion and remediation of pending follow-ups relating to client lifecycle activities
Approve clients and business introducers new accounts, oversee KYC lifecycle and periodic reviews within the authority delegated by HoPB, ensuring oversight and compliance standards are met
Act as a key link between FLOD and SLOD, ensuring consistent communication and escalation paths including networking with key contact persons within the organization

Review account openings, CIP modifications and periodic reviews for the Dubai PBS team, while providing guidance to strengthen their understanding of requirements and control standards. Through continuous support and best practice sharing, help the team develop the capability to perform these reviews independently.
To represent the HOPBS and the Team with credibility, whether deputizing in meetings, presenting to senior stakeholders, or managing complex reviews.
Skills and experience
University degree in Business Administration, Finance, Law, Economics or related field is essential;
Minimum 5 years of previous Compliance or Audit experience in the area of account openings, account reviews and transaction monitoring;
Strong analytical skills;
Strong communication skills, including the ability to deal with potentially contentious issues effectively;
Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment, flexible, multitask;
Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English. Working knowledge of French and German would be an advantage.

University degree in Business Administration, Finance, Law, Economics or related field is essential;
Minimum 5 years of previous Compliance or Audit experience in the area of account openings, account reviews and transaction monitoring;
Strong analytical skills;
Strong communication skills, including the ability to deal with potentially contentious issues effectively;
Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment, flexible, multitask;
Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English. Working knowledge of French and German would be an advantage.

Staying abreast of key technical developments and trends (e.g. AI, cloud technologies) and assessing their relevance for the Group’s data and analytics landscape

summary: The position of Operational Risk Analyst sits within the Operational Risk function and focuses on strengthening the bank’s risk management and internal control framework globally. Acting as a key risk partner to the first line of defence, the position provides independent challenge and oversight on operational risks, ensuring that processes, systems and activities are managed in line with the bank’s risk appetite.
Main responsibilities
Skills and experience
responsibilities: Oversee the effective and consistent implementation and maintenance of the risk and control management framework globally.

Contribute to thematic risk and control assessments on a broad range of issues, including back and middle-office, funds, securities, payments, corporate actions, and order execution through collaboration with other stakeholders.
Perform process-level walkthroughs and control testing to enable the identification and assessment of risks and controls.

Proactively escalate and effectively communicate material control weaknesses, closely collaborate with key stakeholders in the relevant functions to define appropriate action plans and oversee their timely implementation.
Provide expertise and make recommendations for improvements to steer adherence to internal regulations, regulatory requirements and industry best practices.
Master’s degree in Business Administration, Economics, or a technical field (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics).

At least 5 to 7 years of experience in the banking sector (ideally in Risk Control, Audit, or Business Risk Management), or in consulting/audit with a leading professional services firm; a combination of both is considered an advantage.
Strong understanding of banking processes and systems, products, and related regulatory requirements. Expertise with core banking systems and Front systems is a plus.
Excellent communication skills in English, both verbal and written. Knowledge of German, French, or Italian is a plus.
Proven ability to write reports and communicate effectively with a senior audience.
Self-motivated, diligent, highly organized, able to prioritize and multi-task, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, able to work autonomously and as part of a global team.
Innovative mindset, with the ability to identify opportunities for process improvement and operational efficiency.

Perform process-level walkthroughs and control testing to enable the identification and assessment of risks and controls.
